title: Hypotheses, Riskiest Assumptions, and Disposable Prototypes · Cumulative Product Review
summary: A delayed review reconstructs hypotheses, riskiest assumptions, and disposable prototypes from stakeholder, decision, assumption, revision, artifact, observation, changed case, rejected failure, repair, owner, and residual-risk evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for ranked assumption map and prototype evidence after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Express desirable, usable, feasible, viable, ethical, accessible, secure, and operable assumptions as falsifiable claims.
Prioritize assumptions by uncertainty, consequence, dependency, and cheapest discriminating evidence.
Choose paper, clickable, coded, data, integration, or operational prototype fidelity to answer one question without production overinvestment.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-1 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-1
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Assumption Register
steps.instruction:
Order the Assumption Register evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Assumption Register.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Assumption Register assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Assumption Register, keep Assumption Register connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: A polished coded prototype appears persuasive, but it tests no predeclared assumption and quietly becomes production code after one positive comment. The review variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Express desirable, usable, feasible, viable, ethical, accessible, secure, and operable assumptions as falsifiable claims.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Assumption Register has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: For Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Assumption Register, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Assumption Register precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Assumption Register, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A polished coded prototype appears persuasive, but it tests no predeclared assumption and quietly becomes production code after one positive comment. The review variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Express desirable, usable, feasible, viable, ethical, accessible, secure, and operable assumptions as falsifiable claims.

For Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Assumption Register, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A risk list that names topics without predictions is testable.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints for the review Assumption Register case
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure for the review Assumption Register case
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence for the review Assumption Register case
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise for the review Assumption Register case

steps.title: Record what required effort · Review Prototype Disposal
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Prototype Disposal: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A polished coded prototype appears persuasive, but it tests no predeclared assumption and quietly becomes production code after one positive comment. The review variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Separate disposable exploration from production code and record what can and cannot transfer after the experiment.

Evidence target: Record what required effort · Review Prototype Disposal.
steps.why: Retrieving Prototype Disposal through the concrete ranked assumption map and prototype evidence case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Record what required effort · Review Prototype Disposal, keep Prototype Disposal connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Separate disposable exploration from production code and record what can and cannot transfer after the experiment.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The decision applies Assumption Register to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Assumption Register.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using separate and disposable plus specific case evidence.
